stemHtml: How might icing encountered on the ground, rather than in the air, pose more of a threat to an aircraft operation?

options.a: It can occur in places that would normally remain free of icing, such as in wheel wells during taxiing.
options.b: It collects more on the leading edges of the lift-producing surfaces.
options.c: De-icing fluid tends to remain on critical flight surfaces when the aircraft is on the ground and may become a hazard.
options.d: Any rain coming down has more of a chance to turn into drizzle and therefore be more difficult to remove.
